title: Sibling Fights All Day? Use Fair Rules Instead of Constant Policing
excerpt: Cut daily conflict with clear family rules that both children understand and respect.
coverAlt: Two siblings sharing toys on floor

content.text: From Referee Mode to Skill-Building Mode
If sibling fights are nonstop, parents become exhausted referees. The fix is not louder correction; it is clearer rules plus repeatable repair.
Children need short shared rules and a consistent conflict process. This builds fairness and reduces emotional escalation.
When repair becomes routine, frequency and intensity of fights usually drop over time.

items: Use timer-based turns for high-value toys.
End with one repair action every time.

content.text: Less policing, more coaching. That is how sibling conflict becomes growth instead of daily chaos.
